questions.q: Explain the term doping in sports. What are the reasons behind the use of doping in sports? Discuss the role of WADA in preventing doping.
questions.answer: Doping in sports refers to the use of performance-enhancing drugs or methods to gain an unfair advantage over others. The reasons behind the use of doping in sports include the desire to win at all costs, pressure to perform, and the lure of financial rewards. The World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) plays a crucial role in preventing doping by setting standards for doping control, providing education and awareness programs, and monitoring athlete compliance with anti-doping regulations.

questions.q: A sports person is found to have a urine sample with a testosterone level of 20 ng/ml. If the threshold limit is 15 ng/ml, calculate the percentage increase in the testosterone level. What action will be taken against the sports person?
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Percentage increase = ((20 - 15) / 15) * 100 = 33.33%
The sports person will be banned from participating in sports events for a certain period, depending on the severity of the offense.
marking_scheme: Action against the sports person (3 marks)

questions.q: What are the different types of doping methods used in sports? Discuss the harmful effects of doping on the health of sports persons.
questions.answer: The different types of doping methods used in sports include the use of anabolic steroids, stimulants, and human growth hormone. The harmful effects of doping on the health of sports persons include liver damage, cardiovascular problems, and mental health issues. Doping can also lead to long-term health consequences, such as infertility and increased risk of certain cancers.

questions.q: If a sports person is tested positive for a banned substance, what is the procedure to be followed? Calculate the time period for which the sports person will be banned, if the first-time offense carries a ban of 2 years and the second-time offense carries a ban of 4 years.
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Let's assume the sports person has committed a first-time offense.
Time period of ban = 2 years
If the sports person commits a second-time offense, the time period of ban will be 4 years.
The procedure to be followed includes notifying the sports person, providing an opportunity for a hearing, and imposing a ban or other penalty.
marking_scheme: Calculation of time period of ban (2 marks)

questions.q: Explain the importance of education and awareness programs in preventing doping in sports. Discuss the role of coaches, trainers, and support staff in promoting anti-doping practices.
questions.answer: Education and awareness programs are essential in preventing doping in sports as they provide sports persons with the knowledge and skills to make informed decisions about their health and performance. Coaches, trainers, and support staff play a crucial role in promoting anti-doping practices by providing guidance, support, and encouragement to sports persons.
marking_scheme: Importance of education and awareness programs (2 marks)
Role of coaches, trainers, and support staff (3 marks)

questions.q: A sports organization has 100 athletes, and 5 of them are found to be using performance-enhancing drugs. If the organization decides to ban the athletes for a period of 2 years, calculate the percentage of athletes banned. What measures can be taken to prevent doping in the sports organization?
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Percentage of athletes banned = (5 / 100) * 100 = 5%
Measures to prevent doping include implementing strict anti-doping policies, providing education and awareness programs, and conducting regular testing and monitoring.
marking_scheme: Calculation of percentage of athletes banned (2 marks)
